key_insight: CerViX-Net is a hybrid deep-learning system that fuses ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0, and a modified Vision Transformer to improve cervical cytology image classification.
clinical_relevance: If validated in real-world workflows, this approach could support faster, more accurate cervical cancer screening from Pap smear images.
method: ResNet50 branch extracts hierarchical convolutional features.
Modified ViT branch models patch-level context with transformer encoders.
EfficientNet-B0 branch captures multi-scale representations; all branches are fused for classification.
evidence_signal: On the Mendeley LBC dataset, the model reported 99.69% accuracy, 99.28% precision, 99.48% recall, and a 99.52% F1-score, with supportive validation on SIPaKMeD and Herlev Pap Smear datasets.
watch_item: The abstract does not provide details on external prospective validation, class imbalance handling, or how the model performs in routine clinical deployment.
paper_title: CerViX-Net: A Multi-Branch Fusion of Vision Transformer and Convolutional Neural Networks for Cervical Cancer Detection using Cytology Images
